human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for government and public-sector public-compliance boundary discipline, source quality, statutory authority awareness, records and procurement handling, due-process escalation, functional coverage, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to public compliance obligation mapping, evidence handling, official-record readiness, control readiness, exception escalation, and review preparation.

Authority sources cover APA, FOIA, Privacy Act, NARA/eCFR records rules, FAR procurement file rules, 2 CFR Part 200 federal award and audit requirements, OMB A-123 internal control guidance, and GAO Green Book and Yellow Book standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope control mapping, missing official records, conflicting policies, legal signoff refusal, rights-sensitive exceptions, public records and privacy boundaries, procurement file gaps, low-confidence prerequisites, tenant-specific adaptation, operational bypasses, adjudication and enforcement boundaries, review preparation, and policy or financial approval boundaries.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/government-and-public-sector/public-compliance-specialist/ so public compliance work is distinct from program adjudication, legal, public records, procurement, audit, privacy, enforcement, finance, and executive approval lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around legal interpretation, regulator-facing action, formal signoff, official adjudication, enforcement, public-records determination, procurement award, audit opinion, financial approval, policy override, and executive authority.
Functionality coverage is complete across all shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and government-and-public-sector abilities.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated government and public-sector public-compliance specialist for evidence-backed obligation mapping, official-record readiness, control readiness, due-process escalation, procurement and records handling, and review preparation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
